mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate that has risen for six or more consecutive months signals late-stage monetary tightening that raises the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as valuations compress.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 4.29% in Jan 2006 after multi-year tightening cycle since 2004
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and 20d forward returns stays below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or returns following rate increases are not significantly more negative than baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When 3-month annualized PAYEMS growth decelerates below 1.0% after a period of stronger job creation, it signals an emerging labor market slowdown that precedes earnings downgrades, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135,427 thousand (revised) as of Jan 2006, near full employment
failure_condition: Median 20d forward return following triggered deceleration episodes is not significantly below the unconditional sample mean (t-test p > 0.05) across at least three distinct trigger episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts (HOUST) occurring simultaneously with an elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates that monetary tightening is actively suppressing residential investment, foreshadowing a broader demand slowdown that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1994 thousand units (revised) Dec 2005 amid FEDFUNDS 4.29%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with housing sensitivity
failure_condition: Joint HOUST-decline/high-FEDFUNDS episodes fail to produce 60d forward returns below the unconditional median in at least 60% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive power versus HOUST alone (delta Rank IC < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: During a late-cycle expansion, sustained positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) signals continued corporate capacity utilization and earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 98.2 (revised, 2017=100 basis) Jan 2006 reflecting continued expansion
failure_condition: INDPRO positive-growth months are not followed by significantly higher 20d forward returns than negative-growth months (t-test p > 0.05) over rolling 3-year windows, or the effect reverses sign once expansion matures into recession.

mechanism_narrative: When headline CPI year-over-year inflation crosses above 3.5%, it raises expectations of continued Federal Reserve tightening, triggering a short-term risk-off repricing in equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 198.1 (revised) Dec 2005 implying YoY inflation near 3.4-3.6%
failure_condition: 5d forward returns following CPI breach episodes are not significantly negative (p > 0.05) or the signal fails to outperform a random baseline across at least 10 historical trigger events.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low and stable unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 5% signals a healthy labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 4.7% Jan 2006, near cycle lows
failure_condition: Rank IC between low-UNRATE regime indicator and 60d forward returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns during low-unemployment periods are statistically indistinguishable from the full-sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: When retail sales (RSAFS) continue to grow month-over-month even as the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains elevated, it indicates resilient consumer demand that offsets tightening headwinds, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 349,664 million (revised) Jan 2006 with FEDFUNDS at 4.29%
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with resilient consumer
failure_condition: Joint positive-RSAFS/high-FEDFUNDS episodes do not produce 20d forward returns exceeding the unconditional mean in at least 55% of instances, or the interaction adds no explanatory power beyond RSAFS alone.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in real GDP growth (GDPC1) below trend while the federal funds rate remains near cycle highs signals that monetary tightening has begun to constrain aggregate supply capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter annualized growth falls below 2.0% while FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 revised value 16136.734 billion (2005Q4 vintage context) alongside FEDFUNDS 4.29%
failure_condition: GDP deceleration episodes under high FEDFUNDS fail to produce below-median 60d forward returns in at least 60% of cases, or GDPC1 revisions materially reverse the deceleration signal in over half of instances.
